title: Decadent Molten Polish Potato Dumplings
description: A salty and nostalgic Polish dish featuring white chocolate chips, this recipe is a unique twist on traditional potato dumplings, made with whole30-friendly ingredients and a molten center.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 400 degrees Fahrenheit.
In a large bowl, combine the grated potatoes, almond flour, egg, salt, and garlic powder, and mix until a dough forms.
Knead the dough for 5 minutes until it is smooth and pliable.
Divide the dough into 8 equal pieces and roll each piece into a ball.
Flatten each ball into a disk shape and place a few white chocolate chips in the center of each disk.
Fold the dough over the chocolate chips to form a half-moon shape and press the edges together to seal.
Place the dumplings on a baking sheet lined with parchment paper and brush with coconut oil.
Bake the dumplings for 20 minutes, or until they are golden brown and the chocolate is melted.
Serve the dumplings hot and enjoy.
